strengths: Deep distributed-systems and high-throughput backend experience, including low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby.
Hands-on, recent exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, demonstrating practical engagement with the core technical domain.
Broad polyglot background across event-driven architectures, real-time systems, and embedded contexts, with a consistent pattern of owning complex technical problems end-to-end.
concerns: No documented engineering leadership or team-scaling experience; all roles are individual contributor (Senior/Staff SWE) with no evidence of building or managing engineering organizations of any size.

AI agent production experience is nascent and contributor-level — participating in agentic tooling at re:cinq for ~2 months is far short of the role's requirement for demonstrated multi-agent systems ownership in high-stakes environments.
Zero fintech infrastructure depth at the required level — no credit systems, payment flows, real-time credit risk, or regulatory compliance experience surfaced across 15+ years of history.
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically capable and experienced individual contributor with genuine strengths in distributed systems and a recent pivot toward agentic AI tooling, but the gap between his profile and this CTO role is fundamental rather than incremental. The role demands a proven executive who has scaled engineering organizations, owned multi-agent AI systems in production at a consequential level, and operated within regulated fintech environments — none of which appear in his history. His trajectory is that of a deepening senior technologist, not an engineering leader, and placing him in an executive seat overseeing a high-stakes agentic fintech platform would represent a significant and unvalidated leap.
